name: Tomato Bruschetta
tagline: Charred bread rubbed with garlic, piled with tomato, basil, olive oil
highlight: Five ingredients, summer in a single bite

hero.alt: Bruschetta toasts piled with diced tomato and basil
hero.caption: bruschetta — summer on toast

healthFacts: Salting tomatoes 5 min before topping pulls out water and concentrates flavor.

steps.title: Salt the tomatoes
steps.detail: Dice tomatoes, toss with salt and a tablespoon of olive oil. Rest 10 min — concentrates flavor and creates a juicy oil pool.
steps.tip: Don't seed the tomatoes — the gel is the flavor.

steps.title: Toast bread dark
steps.detail: Brush bread slices with olive oil. Grill or broil until charred at the edges — needs the structure.
steps.tip: Char comes from a hot dry surface — pre-heat your grill or broiler.

steps.title: Rub with garlic
steps.detail: While bread is hot, rub a halved raw garlic clove across each slice — it'll grate into the toast.
steps.tip: Hot toast = garlic dissolves into the surface; cold toast = garlic just sits there.

steps.title: Pile and serve
steps.detail: Spoon tomato mixture onto toasts; tear basil over. Drizzle olive oil, crack pepper. Eat immediately — bruschetta sogs out fast.
steps.tip: Plate the tomato bowl alongside instead of pre-loading toasts to keep them crisp.
